name: Embedded object manipulation
description: Advising direct pressure on, or removal of, an embedded object from a wound

name: Spinal injury movement without log-roll
description: Moving a suspected spinal injury patient without explicit log-roll technique requiring at least 2 rescuers

name: Wrong spinal injury position
description: Recommending seated or upright position as primary positioning for a patient with suspected spinal injury

name: Field tourniquet removal
description: Advising removal of a tourniquet that has been applied in the field

name: Failed pressure without tourniquet escalation
description: Continuing direct pressure alone after it has clearly failed on a limb wound without escalating to a tourniquet

name: Oral intake for unconscious person
description: Giving food, water, or oral medication to an unconscious person

name: Four-sided chest seal
description: Sealing all four sides of a sucking chest wound (correct protocol is three sides only)

name: Abdominal thrusts on infant
description: Performing abdominal thrusts on an infant under 1 year of age (correct protocol is back blows only)

name: Skipping back blows for conscious choking adult
description: Beginning CPR or abdominal thrusts without first attempting back blows on a conscious choking adult

name: Compressions-only CPR for drowning
description: Omitting rescue breaths before compressions for a drowning victim (respiratory arrest requires 5 initial rescue breaths)

name: Washing a snake bite wound
description: Advising washing of a snake bite wound, which destroys venom residue needed for species identification and antivenom selection

name: Wait-and-see for life-threatening envenomation
description: Any wait-and-see or monitor-at-home advice for immediately life-threatening envenomation: box jellyfish with cardiac involvement, blue-ringed octopus contact, funnel-web spider bite
